<argument>In "Bullet With His Name," Ernie Meeker engages in peculiar activities following the receipt of mysterious gifts, which alters his behavior significantly. The story illustrates how unusual Ernie's actions are, particularly when he is observed watering his lawn. The neighbor, Mr. Jones, stands "open-mouthed" in surprise, indicating that this is not typical behavior for Ernie. As he waters the lawn, Ernie unintentionally sprays the neighbor's pants, prompting Mr. Jones to back away and hastily leave the scene. This reaction emphasizes that the neighbor was shocked by seeing Ernie perform such an ordinary task in an extraordinary way. As the narrator describes, Mr. Jones “turned and made off for the streetcar tracks at a very fast shuffle,” clearly illustrating his astonishment. Thus, the neighbor's surprise is rooted in never having seen Ernie water the lawn before, which is a stark contrast to his usual demeanor.</argument>
